question: Will CPI rise more than 1.0% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the BLS on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase on a seasonally adjusted basis. Even conflicting higher estimates suggest at most 0.9% monthly growth. Since neither the official 0.3% nor alternative estimates exceed the 1.0% threshold required for YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows 0.3% monthly CPI increase
risks: Market may not update quickly to reflect released data
